name: Els Bessons - Ruta Circular — Les Borges Blanques
region_intro: Mountain biking around Les Borges Blanques offers diverse terrain situated between the central plain of the Catalan depression and the initial foothills of the pre-coastal ranges. The region is characterized by extensive olive groves and gentle hills, providing varied landscapes for exploration. Unique sandstone formations, known as Arenisca Floresta, contribute to the distinct character of the terrain. This setting provides a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Les Borges Blanques The most popular mountain bike trail is Els Bessons - Ruta Circular — Les Borges Blanques, a 21.5 miles (34.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Els Bessons Summit – Los Bessons Olive Groves loop from les Borges Blanques, a difficult 32.7 miles (52.7 km) path. This route ascends to Els Bessons Summit and traverses through olive groves. Local mountain bikers also love the Parque del Terrall – Los Bessons Olive Groves loop from les Borges Blanques, a 27.8 miles (44.7 km) trail leading through Parque del Terrall and Los Bessons Olive Groves, often completed in about 4 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around Les Borges Blanques is defined by olive groves, gentle hills, and the unique Arenisca Floresta sandstone. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Les Borges Blanques are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 11 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Les Borges Blanques's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I expect in Les Borges Blanques?
A: Les Borges Blanques offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from easy singletracks to more challenging routes. The terrain is characterized by extensive olive groves, gentle hills, and unique Arenisca Floresta sandstone formations, providing varied landscapes for exploration. You'll find options suitable for different skill levels, including 2 easy, 28 moderate, and 6 difficult trails.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Les Borges Blanques?
A: Yes, several circular mountain bike routes are available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944333311/els-bessons-ruta-circular-les-borges-blanques" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Bessons - Ruta Circular&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 34.6 km and typically takes around 3 hours 15 minutes. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31521506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Bessons Summit – Los Bessons Olive Groves loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 52.7 km route that ascends to Els Bessons Summit.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I discover along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can ride through picturesque olive groves, which are a dominant feature of the landscape. For scenic views, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5175602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque del Terrall&lt;/a&gt;, known as the town's 'green lungs' with a botanical garden and ponds. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14892827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque del Terrall – Els Bessons Summit loop&lt;/a&gt; offers both park scenery and summit views. The Banqueta del Canal d'Urgell also provides additional natural scenery.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike in the area?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls near some of the mountain bike routes. Notable ones include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3776964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Second Machine&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These natural attractions can be a refreshing stop during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Les Borges Blanques?
A: Les Borges Blanques generally offers good conditions for riding, with the overall trail status often reported as 'Clear / Green'. The region's climate, situated between the Catalan depression and pre-coastal ranges, typically provides pleasant weather for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering milder temperatures compared to the heat of summer.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the area does offer 2 easy trails among its 36 routes. These easier options, often through the gentle olive groves, would be more suitable for families or beginners. Always check the route's difficulty and distance before heading out with children.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Les Borges Blanques?
A: The mountain bike trails in Les Borges Blanques are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 11 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the extensive olive groves, and the unique geological features like the Arenisca Floresta sandstone that contribute to a distinct riding experience.

Q: Can I combine mountain biking with cultural or historical exploration?
A: Absolutely. Les Borges Blanques has a rich cultural heritage. You can explore the historic center with its porticoed square, visit the Macià Space, or see the old Cal Gineret oil mill. The Parish Church of the Assumption also offers views of the Pyrenees from its bell tower. Consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14946954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arbeca Castle – Parque del Terrall loop&lt;/a&gt;, which can lead you near historical sites.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are several longer and more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31521506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Bessons Summit – Los Bessons Olive Groves loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 52.7 km path with significant elevation gain. Another substantial route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33785968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque del Terrall – Los Bessons Olive Groves loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 44.7 km.

Q: What is the terrain like on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain is quite varied, reflecting the region's position between the Catalan depression and the pre-coastal ranges. You'll encounter gentle hills, extensive olive groves, and areas featuring the unique Arenisca Floresta sandstone. This geological feature, formed from compacted sand and minerals, adds a distinct character to the trails.

Q: Are there any specific parks or natural areas to explore by bike?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5175602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque del Terrall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural area within the town, featuring a botanical garden and natural ponds. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33785968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque del Terrall – Los Bessons Olive Groves loop&lt;/a&gt; incorporate this park into the riding experience, offering a mix of natural beauty and cycling.
tips:
Nice park in the center of Les Borges Blanques, in Lleida.
I recommend a walk there, where we can see animals, such as fish, ducks or a beautiful swan. There is a playground and green areas to play.
If we want to go around the interior, we must do it on foot, since it is not allowed by bicycle.

Los Bessons, is an area near the town of Les Borges Blanques, popular for lovers of mountain biking or hiking in the middle of traditional rainfed crops.
The name (Bessons, twins in Catalan) comes from the fact that there are two equal hills that we will easily see as they stand out in the area.
